name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Northern Arabian Sea — repositioned off eastern Oman coast, ~1,100km from Iran
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
notes: Day 25: Sole operational carrier in direct theater. Active combat ops: F/A-18 launches, Tomahawk strikes ongoing. CSG-3 with CVW-9, DESRON 21. CENTCOM updated Mar 24: 9,000+ targets struck, 140+ vessels destroyed, 9,000+ combat flights. USS Mitscher (DDG-57) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) active in Persian Gulf/Strait patrols. USS Spruance (DDG-111) fired Tomahawks from Arabian Sea.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23, CENTCOM 3/24, CBS News, CNBC

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Naval Support Activity Souda Bay, Crete — pierside emergency fire repairs
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Arrived Souda Bay, Crete Mar 23 for emergency pierside repairs — at least 1 week — 270+ days deployed
notes: Day 25: At NSA Souda Bay, Crete for pierside repairs. Arrived Mar 23 following non-combat fire Mar 12 in aft laundry facility. 1+ week expected. 600+ sailors displaced; 1,000 mattresses shipped from CVN-79 Kennedy. 272 days deployed, nearing post-Vietnam record. Navy says 'fully mission capable.' Escorts USS Bainbridge (DDG-96) and USS Mahan (DDG-72) remain with Ford.
source: USNI News, Navy official announcement, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Naval Station Norfolk, VA — NOT yet deployed
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Awaiting deployment — needs minor maintenance and pre-deployment leave — earliest departure ~March 30
notes: Day 25: Deployment-certified (COMPTUEX Mar 5); CSG-10/CVW-7 ready at Norfolk. Widely expected for Med/ME deployment but NO confirmed orders as of Mar 24. CO Bibeau: 'When they need us…we are ready.' Creating rare potential 3-carrier Middle East posture if deployed.
source: USNI News (Mar 6), Navy Times, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/23

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Arabian Sea — entering CENTCOM AOR ~March 25 per WSJ
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53K King Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 25: WSJ confirmed (Mar 24) Tripoli ARG enters CENTCOM AOR approximately March 25 (Friday). Several additional days to reach Hormuz area. Diego Garcia as of Mar 23 per USNI Fleet Tracker. ~2,200 Marines (31st MEU), ~20 F-35Bs. Most significant force addition since war began.
source: WSJ (Mar 24), USNI Fleet Tracker (Mar 23), Reuters, USNI News

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: CRITICAL: USS Tulsa/Santa Barbara in Singapore as of Mar 18 — USS Canberra location uncertain — MCM gap severe
vessels: LCS-30 USS Canberra (Indian Ocean — location uncertain)
LCS-16 USS Tulsa (Singapore as of Mar 18)
LCS-32 USS Santa Barbara (Singapore as of Mar 18)
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz — SEVERELY DEGRADED
notes: Day 24: USS Tulsa (LCS-16) and USS Santa Barbara (LCS-32) confirmed in Singapore for maintenance — not in the Gulf. USS Canberra (LCS-30) location undisclosed. Four decommissioned Avenger-class MCMs arrived Philadelphia in January for scrapping. Result: near-zero dedicated mine countermeasures capability in the Persian Gulf despite Iran's estimated 5,000-6,000 mine arsenal.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Washington Institute, Christian Science Monitor 3/20, CNN 3/11, Naval News

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Eastern Pacific — departed San Diego March 19-20, en route to Middle East
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid reinforcement — originally scheduled Indo-Pacific, rerouted and accelerated to Middle East
notes: Day 25: Departed San Diego Mar 18-19 in 'accelerated deployment.' Currently transiting Pacific — ETA theater early-to-mid April (~3 weeks from departure). 11th MEU embarked (~2,200 Marines), F-35Bs. Combined with Tripoli ARG arrival, will bring ~4,400 additional Marines to theater. Indo-Pacific coverage gap: only CVN-73 George Washington (in maintenance at Yokosuka) remains in Pacific.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker (Mar 23), AP, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon

name: HMS Anson
mission_summary: UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
notes: Day 23: Astute-class nuclear-powered attack submarine. In Arabian Sea. UK also dispatched 2 additional warships toward Gulf. UK military planners embedded at CENTCOM Tampa. UK approved US use of Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford for 'specific and limited defensive operations' against Iranian missile sites following Diego Garcia IRBM attack.
